company.what_they_do: Advanced Hospitality Technologies (ADVH Tech) is a specialized IT managed services and technology consulting firm exclusively focused on the hospitality sector. They serve hotels, resorts, restaurants, and event venues — helping clients build, integrate, and maintain secure IT ecosystems. Services span: technology strategy/roadmapping (vendor-agnostic), system integration (PMS, POS, CRM, IoT, mobile room keys, contactless guest solutions), 24/7 managed IT support (proactive monitoring, remote resolution, onsite technicians), compliance management (PCI DSS, PII, GDPR, HIPAA, brand standards for Marriott/Hilton/etc.), vendor management and procurement, new-build IT infrastructure buildouts, and legacy system modernization. They also operate a hardware shop (shop.advhtech.com). Three stated core pillars: Risk & Compliance Management, Back Office Management, and Guest Technology Experience.
raw_notes: Founded 1996 per About page (advhtech.com/about.php); LinkedIn lists founded 2011 — discrepancy to note.
Headquartered in Fremont, California (LinkedIn, Crunchbase, ZoomInfo all confirm).
Company size: LinkedIn lists 51–200 employees; Crunchbase lists 501–1000; SignalHire lists 13 — figures are inconsistent across sources, treat with caution.
Microsoft Gold Partner and certified PCI Compliant solution provider (advhtech.com/about.php).
Tagline/positioning: 'Secure, Simply & Standardize Technology' — three Ss used prominently on homepage.
Serves properties ranging from 30-room boutiques to 3,000+ room multi-property chains/management companies.
Maintains direct escalation channels with third-party providers (PMS, POS, credit card processors, GPNS, GRE, phones).
Offers brand standards compliance for major flags (Marriott, Hilton, and others).
Has a podcast: 'Reshape Hospitality with Tech' (LinkedIn posts).
Hardware shop at shop.advhtech.com sells Dell, Microsoft Surface, StarTech, and other hardware — suggests possible procurement/resale arm.
Blog exists at advhtech.com/blog/ covering hospitality technology topics.
Crunchbase describes them as having an 'award winning benchmark' for hospitality IT — no specific award named or sourced.

Technology consulting page (advhtech.com/technology-consulting-for-hospitality/) emphasizes vendor-agnostic recommendations and covers Select Service, Preferred Service, Full Service, and Resort property tiers.
Services include: cabling, WiFi, surveillance, mobile keys, in-room controls (lights/HVAC/entertainment), digital signage, guest internet/RADIUS network systems, managed business center.
